title: 三只瞎老鼠
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\151-三只瞎老鼠-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Betty Gregg expresses her frustrations to Mr. Parker Pyne regarding Basil’s tendency to prioritize his mother’s influence and his perceived dullness due to being pampered. Mr. Pyne conducts a brief compatibility test with Betty, asking about her sleeping habits, food preferences, and bedtime; he concludes that she and Basil are well-suited. During this time, Mr. Pyne advises Mrs. Chester to act reluctantly regarding her son's engagement to maintain leverage.

After a week-long vacation in Soleil, Mr. Pyne returns to the Pinewood Hotel to find Mrs. Chester and a pale, tearful Miss Betsey drinking tea. Their conversation is interrupted by Basil, who arrives from the beach accompanied by a glamorous, dark-skinned woman named Delores (also referred to as Miss Ramona or Miss Lamorna). Delores, dressed in light blue chiffon with heavy makeup, dismisses the other women and calls Basil "darling." She subsequently leads Basil away to her hotel, leaving the remaining group in an awkward silence.

Following their departure, Betty leaves in anger, and Mrs. Chester expresses regret over the trip and her son's behavior. Mr. Pyne attempts to reassure Mrs. Chester, suggesting that Delores's presence has at least broken Basil's previous infatuation with Betty. When Basil returns, he defends his right to have friends and dismisses his mother's concerns about Betty's jealousy. A few days later, Basil breaks his scheduled plans for a picnic with Betty to go to Fontainebleau with Delores and her friends.
